Caption: (a) Cross-sectional and (b) top view of anode depicting the coating constituents and a layer of Platinum deposited on the surface inside FIB/SEM (Images captured with NOVA 600 by Dr. Prasath Babu Revathy Rajan, KTH Royal Institute of Technology, Stockholm, Sweden).
(a) Test set-up with electrode sample glued to faceplates. The anode coating is subjected to (b) Tension and (c) Compression.
The geometry of the electrode in bending.

U-shape bending tests with unload steps for tension (width = 9.99 mm) and compression (width = 10.10 mm) configuration (a) Force vs. 1 / Δ 2 with a linear fit to unload curves denoted by dashed lines (b) Root mean square (RMS) of average strain vs. 1 / Δ 2 (c) Zoomed view at 1 / Δ 2 = 1375 m − 2 –2045 m − 2 (d) Zoomed view at 1 / Δ 2 = 2150 m − 2 –3250 m − 2 .
Elastic moduli of anode coating calculated at different bending strains with varying displacement rates in (a) compression and (b) tension.
Stress relaxation tests in tension and compression configurations with faceplate separation (Δ) 15 mm kept constant for 6 h and the corresponding Prony series fit.
Elastic moduli of anode (Fig. 5) and cathode coatings [14] at a displacement rate of 30 mm/min, (a) compression, and (b) tension.
Images showing distinct morphology of active particles and binders for (a) anode and (b) cathode. The surfaces are polished using Broad-ion-beam (BIB) and the images are taken using SEM.
